title: Bridge Essay: The origins and development of aesthetics.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** (Phase 5) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Water/Pleasure to mechanistic neuroscience.

**The origins and development of aesthetics.** — Philos Trans R Soc Lond B Biol Sci. PMID: 39005026. DOI: 10.1098/rstb.2023.0246. All people (and some other animals) have aesthetic responses to sensory stimulation, responses of emotional pleasure or displeasure. These emotions vary from one person and culture to another, yet they share acommon mechanism. To survive, an adaptive animal (as opposed to atropic animal) needs to become comfortable with normality and to have slight abnormalities draw attention to themselves.
